meaning: vt.更改结构,重建构造,调整,改组
examples: Since technology has such an insatiable appetite for eating up human jobs, this phenomenon will continue to restructure our economy in ways we cannot immediately foresee.
The operations can be quite involved, requiring many procedures in order to restructure the anatomy.
Countless studies demonstrate that people can restructure their emotional state using emotion-refocusing techniques.
Debt continues to mount as a lack of political agreement fails to restructure government spending.
"My clear intention is to substantially reshape and restructure the company, " he said in a statement.
